title: Beam Search in Translation
concept: Beam search prevents models from getting stuck on early bad word choices, significantly improving translation quality. Boss explain ends with: 'Next up: Teacher Forcing.'

exercises.prompt: What is the main problem with translating a sentence word-by-word using a simple 'greedy'by-word' without looking ahead?

options.text: It can get stuck on an early bad choice.
It is too slow to use on modern computers.

exercises.explain: Translating word-by-word without looking ahead often leads to awkward or incorrect sentences because early mistakes ruin the rest of the text.

cards.title: Don't Get Stuck!
cards.body: Have you ever translated a sentence word-by-word, only to realize the ending makes no sense because you messed up the start? Translating AIs do this too! Beam search is the superpower that helps them look ahead.

cards.title: What is Beam Search?
cards.body: Beam search is a smart search strategy used in language translation. Instead of picking just the single best word at each step, it keeps track of a set number of top-ranking word sequences to find the overall best translation.

cards.title: The Maze Runners
cards.body: Think of translation like navigating a maze. A greedy search only looks at the immediate next turn, often getting trapped in a dead end. Beam search keeps a small team of runners (the beam width) exploring a few promising paths at the same time, finding the true way out.

cards.title: Tracking Options
cards.body: When translating a sentence, if the AI starts with 'The', it doesn't just guess one next word. It might keep 'The cat' and 'The dog' as its top choices, building on both of them until it sees which full sentence makes the most sense.

cards.title: Myth: AIs Check Every Word
cards.body: Many believe that translation AIs check every single possible sentence combination. That would take forever! Beam search only keeps a few (like 3 or 5) of the best active paths, keeping translation super fast and highly accurate.

cards.title: Greedy vs. Beam Search
cards.body: Compare how Greedy Search and Beam Search explore translation paths. Beam Search avoids dead ends by keeping multiple options open.

left.text: Picks only 1 path. If it starts wrong, the whole translation is ruined.

right.text: Tracks multiple paths. If one path fails, it switches to a better one.

exercises.prompt: How does Beam Search work when translating a sentence?

options.text: It chooses only the single absolute best word at each step.
It keeps track of a set number of highly likely word paths.
It translates the sentence backward from the last word.
It randomly mixes words until they sound correct.

exercises.explain: Beam search keeps track of the top 'N' candidate translations at each step (this number is called the beam width) to avoid getting stuck with a single bad choice.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Greedy Search and Beam Search use the exact same method to choose words, always producing the same translation.
exercises.explain: Beam search keeps multiple promising options open, whereas Greedy Search only picks the single best next word at each step.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete the explanation of beam search.
exercises.sentence: Beam search keeps track of multiple alternative paths instead of choosing just one path.
exercises.explain: Instead of picking just one word and hoping for the best, beam search keeps track of alternative word sequences.

exercises.prompt: Which translation shows the fluid, high-quality output typical of a model using Beam Search?

options.text: The water fell from the cloud and wet me.
It started to rain, soaking my clothes.

exercises.explain: Option B is generated by a simple greedy model that gets stuck on a bad initial word choice ('The water fell'). Option A is a standard dictionary look-up. Option C shows a high-quality translation produced by keeping multiple paths open with beam search.

exercises.prompt: How does Beam Search successfully prevent translation models from getting stuck on poor initial word choices?

options.text: It translates every word at the exact same time.
It only translates the very last word of a sentence.
It keeps a small set of top translations and develops them step-by-step.
It randomly guesses words until one fits.

exercises.explain: Beam search keeps multiple translation paths alive simultaneously, preventing the AI from getting stuck on early mistakes. Next up: Teacher Forcing.
